lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr? Be ye wantin' te go on account with our gang o' fillibusters?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: The powder monkey be takin' a caulk after gettin' rowdy on bumboo, so there be plenty of room for ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiiight...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll just be over here if you need me.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's a landlubber like ye doin' here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Helping Cap'n Teach shoot his way through a little blockade.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well done to ye. I'll see ye around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is this place walled off?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well [lad/lass], it's 'cos there be foul things in the jungle beyond.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of foul things?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Big 'uns, nasty 'uns. You name it, we got it.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: There be Jungle Horrors in the jungles, and Cave Horrors in the caves.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Tis no place te be havin' a picnic and no mistake.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Watch where yer goin'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Then it must have been my fault and I apologise [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I accept your apology. Good day.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Aye, good day te ye too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arr...

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good morning!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I suppose it is. What of it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just striking up a conversation.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well...why would ye do a thing like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No reason.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What ship did ye pull in on, stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Adventurous.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: That's a good ship, that it be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Keep yer voice down [lass/lad], can't ye see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: First off, it's [lad/lass], and second, other than busy being drunk I can't see what you are talking about.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I can't feel me brains no more...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait...I recognise that smell. You've been drinking 'rum' haven't you?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr...ye shufflin' muffle burble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that can be filed as a 'yes'.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Are ye here for...you know...the stuff...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: The stuff...you know...the stuff?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I follow you.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Aren't ye Toothless [Kevin/Lisa]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Sorry, sorry, my mistake!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Ain't ye the scurvy curr that killed my brother?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I kill so many things on a daily basis that it all tends to blur together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me, did they have any unusual features that would help me to recall them?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: How many things de ye have te kill in a day te lose count?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, if I did I wouldn't have lost count!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, about your brother...

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Never mind! I never liked him that much anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's rather quiet around here for a pirate haven isn't it?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr, we like te keep the peace around here.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Some of the older pirates don't like it, but Mos Le'Harmless be a hive of sense and civility.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How nice!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arr, that it be.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering who you are.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, I'm one of the militia, we try and keep the rest of the pirates in line.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, as in line as we reckon they need to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do you decide who becomes a member of the militia?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, if yer ship is in need of repairs and yer in port, then yer a member, simple as that.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: See, those of us that are here for a long haul tend to want to keep things peaceful, so we keep an eye on everyone else as and when they come in.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Given the number of skirmishes we have with customs, there is never a shortage of repairs needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, that was very informative.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just passing through.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: See to it ye keeps outs trouble, stranger.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just looking about.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well good luck with that, landlubber.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Adventuring!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Adventurin' eh...that sounds like customs and excise work to my ears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, I'm a pirate, honest. I have the book and everything.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I'll have me eye on ye, customs [boy/girl]. One step out of line and bam.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: They won't find enough of ye to feed a hungry sea slug.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just looking around. What is there to do here?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, if yeve got some coins in yer purse ye can head over to Joe's house of 'Rum' and get yerself drunk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, Smithin' Smith and Dodgy Mike have some stuff to sell, and ye can always try grabbin' some fish from Two Feet Charley if yer hungry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well, ye can head out to the brewery and have a word with Fancy Dan or San Fan. They always need a hand out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doing what?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Doin' each others heads in most of the time.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What is your business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing much, just passing through.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Well have a good day, [sir/miss].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but you don't sound much like a pirate, how did you come to be here?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I used to be a language tutor, teaching in a small school just outside of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: However the place closed down due to ill attendance, and so I was forced to take up piracy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wasn't that a bit of a big jump in careers?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Not really, I made my way through via a short stint as a lawyer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I must return to my duties. Have a nice day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You too!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's yours?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I asked first!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And very rudely too I might add.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Fine! Go about yer 'business'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will!

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Whaz yer bushinessssss...ssss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you drunk on duty?

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: I'm not drunk! I'll knock down any man as sayin' I'm been having a drinking on my duties!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think you'll have a hard time explaining your slurred speech, odd behaviour, bitter, nostril dissolving stench and rolling, bloodshot eyes if your not drunk.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Arrr, ye got me. I just can't leave the stuff alone.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Cap'n Braindeath's Ten X With Added Cattle Medication just keeps singin' its siren song to me.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Would ye care fer a drop?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I would most certainly not.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Suit yerself.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None of yours.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Fair enough. Off ye go.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: What's yer business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just helping out a friend.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Which friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bill Teach.

lines.speaker: Pirate
lines.text: Bill hired you eh? He must have been scrapin' the barrel and no mistake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! I resent that!
